question: How do students join the Office Hours or live workshop if the Zoom link isn’t public?
answer_agent:
Students don’t join via the public Zoom link.

For Office Hours or live/workshop sessions:
- The **Zoom link is only shared with instructors/presenters/TAs**
- **Students watch on YouTube Live**
- Questions should be sent via **Slido**; the link is pinned in the chat when live
- The session video URL is also posted in the **announcements channel on Telegram/Slack** before it starts
- You can also watch on the **DataTalksClub YouTube channel**

Also, avoid posting questions in the live chat, since they may get missed.

The zoom link is only published to instructors/presenters/TAs.

Students participate via YouTube Live and submit questions to Slido (link is pinned in the chat when live). The video URL should be posted in the [announcements channel on Telegram and Slack](https://t.me/dezoomcamp) before it begins. You can also watch live on the DataTalksClub [YouTube Channel](https://www.youtube.com/c/DataTalksClub).

Don’t post questions in chat as they may be missed if the room is very active.

question: Where should I begin if I want to start LLM Zoomcamp from scratch?
answer_agent:
If you’re starting LLM Zoomcamp from scratch, begin here:

1. Read the [LLM Zoomcamp docs](https://datatalks.club/docs/courses/llm-zoomcamp/)
2. Check the [general Zoomcamp logistics docs](https://datatalks.club/docs/courses/zoomcamp-logistics/)
3. Open the [LLM Zoomcamp GitHub repository](https://github.com/DataTalksClub/llm-zoomcamp)

A good workflow is:
- watch the lesson videos,
- go through the notebooks/code,
- read the homework instructions on GitHub,
- submit your answers on the course platform before the deadline.

You can start anytime, and the current deadlines/progress are on the [course management platform](https://courses.datatalks.club/llm-zoomcamp-2026/).

If you want, I can also give you a “first week plan” for starting the course.

question: How do I find my own entry on the leaderboard if I can’t see my name right away?
answer_agent:
Click the **“Jump to your record on the leaderboard”** link — that will take you directly to your entry, even if your name isn’t visible right away.

If you want to check what name appears publicly, go to **“Edit Course Profile”** and look at your **display name**.
answer_orig:
When you set up your account, you are automatically assigned a random name, such as “Lucid Elbakyan.” Click on the "Jump to your record on the leaderboard" link to find your entry.

If you want to see what your Display name is, click on the "Edit Course Profile" button.

<{IMAGE:image_1}>

- **First field:** This is your nickname/displayed name. You can change it if you want to be known by your Slack username, GitHub username, or any other nickname of your choice. This is useful if you want to remain anonymous.
- **Second field:** Change this to your official name as in your identification documents—passport, national ID card, driver's license, etc. This is mandatory if you do not want "Lucid Elbakyan" on your certificate. This name will appear on your Certificate!

question: Why do I show up with a random name on the leaderboard instead of my actual name?

You’re seeing a random leaderboard name because the course assigns a default anonymous display name when your account is created.

- The **leaderboard uses your display name**, not your certificate name.

question: Can I do the course on my own time and still get the certificate?
answer_agent:
You can work through the course material on your own time, but **you can’t get the certificate fully self-paced**.

To receive a certificate, you need to:
- finish the course with a **live cohort**
- complete the **capstone project**
- do the required **peer reviews**

Homework is **not required** for the certificate, and you can study the material in self-paced mode, but your **project submission and peer review must happen while a live cohort is accepting them**.

Yes — you can work through the material and prepare your project in self-paced mode, but the **project submission and peer review must happen while a live cohort is accepting them**.

So the short answer is:

- **Prepare in self-paced mode:** yes
- **Wait and submit later in a live cohort:** yes
- **Get a certificate without a live cohort submission/peer review:** no

If you want, I can also explain how this affects the capstone and certificate requirements.

No, you can only get a certificate if you finish the course with a "live" cohort.

peer review must happen while a live cohort is accepting them.

- pass the **Capstone project**
- complete the required **peer reviews**
- finish while a **live cohort** is accepting submissions

question: Why does the homework content keep changing before the deadline?
answer_agent:
If the homework title contains `[DRAFT]`, it means the homework is not ready yet.

The homework is only considered ready when both are true:

- The homework form is open on the course management platform.
- The homework title does not contain `[DRAFT]`.

Until then, the content can still change, so working on it early is at your own risk because the final version may be different.
